nodes.label: Loads ~/.hermes/.env with override=True and project .env as fallback; returns list of loaded paths.
Wraps load_dotenv with UTF-8/latin-1 encoding fallback.
Log viewing subcommand with tail, follow, level/session/time filtering for ~/
Main entry point for reading and displaying log lines with optional real-time follow mode.
Reads last N matching lines from a log file, reading more raw lines when filters are active.
Efficiently reads last N lines from a file using chunk-based reading for large files (>1MB).
Polls a log file for new content and prints matching lines in real time.
Prints available log files with sizes and modification times.
Checks if a log line passes level, session, and time-based filters.
Parses relative time strings like '1h', '30m', '2d' into datetime cutoffs.
Extracts and parses timestamp from a log line.
Extracts log level (DEBUG/INFO/WARNING/ERROR/CRITICAL) from a log line.
Gateway subcommand handler: run, start, stop, restart, status, install, uninstall, setup for systemd/launchd services
Main dispatcher for gateway subcommands (run, setup, install, uninstall, start, stop, restart, status).
Runs the gateway in foreground via asyncio.run(start_gateway()).
Interactive setup for 12+ messaging platforms and gateway service installation.
Finds PIDs of running gateway processes via ps/wmic, excluding specified PIDs.
Kills running gateway processes with SIGTERM or SIGKILL.
Stops only the gateway for the current HERMES_HOME profile using PID file.
Generates systemd unit file content for user or system gateway service.
Generates launchd plist XML content for macOS gateway service.
Installs systemd unit, enables it, and ensures linger is configured.
Installs launchd plist and bootstraps it via launchctl.
Starts the systemd gateway service, auto-refreshing unit if stale.
Starts the launchd gateway service via kickstart, self-healing missing plists.
Restarts the systemd gateway service with unit refresh.
Restarts the launchd service using kickstart -k for atomic kill+restart.
Shows systemd service status with linger guidance and runtime health.
Shows launchd service load status, plist freshness, and recent logs.
Returns profile-scoped systemd service name (e.g. hermes-gateway-coder).
Derives service name suffix from HERMES_HOME profile path.
Auto-enables systemd linger so user gateway service survives logout.
Rewrites installed systemd unit when generated definition has changed.
Rewrites installed launchd plist when generated definition has changed.
Interactive setup for Telegram, Discord, Slack, and other standard platforms.
Interactive setup for Signal messenger via signal-cli HTTP daemon.
Returns plain-text configuration status string for a messaging platform.
List of dicts defining 12+ messaging platform configs with env vars and setup instructions.
Shared ANSI color utilities for CLI output with NO_COLOR/TERM=dumb/TTY awareness
ANSI color code constants: RESET, BOLD, DIM, RED, GREEN, YELLOW, BLUE, MAGENTA, CYAN.
Applies ANSI color codes to text when color output is appropriate.
Returns True when colored output is appropriate (respects NO_COLOR, TERM=dumb, TTY).
GitHub Copilot OAuth device code flow and token validation for Copilot API access
Runs GitHub OAuth device code flow for Copilot authentication with polling.
Resolves GitHub token from env vars (COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN, GH_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN) then gh CLI.
Validates token is usable with Copilot API (rejects classic PAT ghp_* tokens).
Builds standard headers for Copilot API requests including Editor-Version and User-Agent.
Returns token from gh auth token CLI when GitHub CLI is available.
Returns PIDs managed by systemd/launchd gateway services to avoid killing them during stale sweeps.
Waits for gateway process to exit by PID file, sending SIGKILL after grace period.
Detects active virtualenv directory from sys.prefix or common names under PROJECT_ROOT.
Returns Python path preferring venv python over system python.
Returns path to hermes CLI binary or falls back to module execution.
Returns systemd linger status for current user (enabled/disabled/unknown).
Dict mapping log names to filenames: agent.log, errors.log, gateway.log.
OAuth client ID for Copilot device code flow (Ov23li8tweQw6odWQebz).
Tuple of env var names checked in order: COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN, GH_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN.
Checks if a token is a classic PAT (ghp_*).

metadata.description: Skills for data science workflows -- interactive exploration, Jupyter notebooks, data analysis, and visualization.
Diagram creation skills for generating visual diagrams, flowcharts, architecture diagrams, and illustrations using tools like Excalidraw.
Skills for sending, receiving, searching, and managing email from the terminal.
Skills for monitoring, aggregating, and processing RSS feeds, blogs, and web content sources.
Skills for setting up, configuring, and managing game servers, modpacks, and gaming-related infrastructure.
Skills for searching, downloading, and working with GIFs and short-form animated media.
GitHub workflow skills for managing repositories, pull requests, code reviews, issues, and CI/CD pipelines using the gh CLI and git via terminal.

properties.name: github-pr-workflow
properties.author: Hermes Agent
properties.description: Full pull request lifecycle -- create branches, commit changes, open PRs, monitor CI status, auto-fix failures, and merge. Works with gh CLI or falls back to git + GitHub REST API via curl.

properties.name: github-repo-management
properties.author: Hermes Agent
properties.description: Clone, create, fork, configure, and manage GitHub repositories. Manage remotes, secrets, releases, and workflows.
GitHub issue template for bug reports with sections for bug description, steps to reproduce, expected/actual behavior, environment, error output, and additional context.
GitHub issue template for feature requests with sections for feature description, motivation, proposed solution, alternatives, scope estimate, and additional context.
Pull request body template for bug fixes with sections for bug description, root cause, fix details, verification steps, test plan, and risk assessment.
Pull request body template for features with sections for summary, motivation, changes, test plan, screenshots/examples, and notes for reviewers.

Common CI failure patterns and diagnosis guide: test failures, lint/formatting failures, type check failures, build failures, permission/auth failures, timeout failures, Docker/container failures, and auto-fix decision tree.
Conventional commit format reference with types (feat, fix, refactor, docs, test, ci, chore, perf, style, build, revert), scope, breaking changes, multi-line body, and issue linking conventions.

Comprehensive GitHub REST API reference covering repositories, pull requests, issues, CI/GitHub Actions, releases, secrets, branch protection, user/auth, pagination, rate limits, and common curl patterns.
Run 150+ AI applications in the cloud via the inference.sh platform. One API key for image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, 3D, social, and audio.

properties.name: find-nearby
properties.description: Find nearby places (restaurants, cafes, bars, pharmacies, etc.) using OpenStreetMap. Works with coordinates, addresses, cities, zip codes, or Telegram location pins. No API keys needed.
Python script for finding nearby places using OpenStreetMap Overpass and Nominatim APIs. Supports geocoding, haversine distance calculation, multiple place types, JSON output, and Google Maps links.

properties.name: mcporter
properties.author: community
properties.description: Use the mcporter CLI to list, configure, auth, and call MCP servers/tools directly (HTTP or stdio), including ad-hoc servers, config edits, and CLI/type generation.

properties.name: native-mcp
properties.author: Hermes Agent
properties.description: Built-in MCP (Model Context Protocol) client that connects to external MCP servers, discovers their tools, and registers them as native Hermes Agent tools. Supports stdio and HTTP transports with automatic reconnection, security filtering, and zero-config tool injection.

properties.name: gif-search
properties.author: Hermes Agent
properties.description: Search and download GIFs from Tenor using curl. No dependencies beyond curl and jq.

properties.name: heartmula
properties.description: Set up and run HeartMuLa, the open-source music generation model family (Suno-like). Generates full songs from lyrics + tags with multilingual support. Includes HeartMuLa (3B/7B), HeartCodec, HeartTranscriptor, HeartCLAP.

properties.name: songsee
properties.author: community
properties.description: Generate spectrograms and audio feature visualizations (mel, chroma, MFCC, tempogram, etc.) from audio files via CLI.

properties.name: youtube-content
properties.description: Fetch YouTube video transcripts and transform them into structured content (chapters, summaries, threads, blog posts).
Python script to fetch YouTube video transcripts as structured JSON. Supports multiple URL formats, language selection, timestamps, and text-only output.
Examples of output formats for YouTube transcript transformation: chapters, summary, chapter summaries, Twitter/X thread, blog post, and quotes.

properties.name: modal-serverless-gpu
properties.author: Orchestra Research
properties.description: Serverless GPU cloud platform for running ML workloads. On-demand GPU access, deploying ML models as APIs, batch jobs with auto-scaling.

Advanced Modal usage: multi-GPU training, DeepSpeed integration, advanced container configuration, lifecycle hooks, concurrent request handling, dynamic batching, volume operations, cloud bucket mounts, function composition, parallel fan-out, web endpoints, WebSocket, streaming, authentication, cost optimization, production deployment, sandboxes.

Troubleshooting guide for Modal: authentication, package installation, container image issues, GPU issues, cold start problems, volume issues, web endpoint errors, CORS, secret management, scheduling issues, debugging tips, common error messages.

properties.name: evaluating-llms-harness
properties.author: Orchestra Research
properties.description: Evaluates LLMs across 60+ academic benchmarks (MMLU, HumanEval, GSM8K, TruthfulQA, HellaSwag). Industry standard used by EleutherAI, HuggingFace, and major labs. Supports HuggingFace, vLLM, APIs.

Guide to evaluating OpenAI, Anthropic, and other API-based language models using lm-evaluation-harness. Covers OpenAI completions/chat, Anthropic completions/chat, local OpenAI-compatible APIs (vLLM, TGI, Ollama, llama.cpp), custom API implementation, cost management, and best practices.

Complete guide to 60+ evaluation tasks in lm-evaluation-harness including MMLU, GSM8K, HumanEval, BBH, IFEval, GLUE, LongBench, TruthfulQA, ARC, HellaSwag, WinoGrande, PIQA, MATH, MBPP, DROP, multilingual benchmarks (AfroBench, NorEval). Includes score interpretation and selection guide.

Guide to creating domain-specific evaluation tasks in lm-evaluation-harness using YAML configuration files with optional Python utilities. Covers output types, data formats, prompt engineering with Jinja2, custom Python functions, filtering, task groups, and real-world examples.

Guide to running evaluation across multiple GPUs using data parallelism, tensor parallelism, and pipeline parallelism. Covers HuggingFace models, vLLM models, NVIDIA NeMo, SGLang, performance comparisons, multi-node evaluation with SLURM, and best practices.

properties.name: weights-and-biases
properties.author: Orchestra Research
properties.description: Track ML experiments with automatic logging, visualize training in real-time, optimize hyperparameters with sweeps, and manage model registry with W&B.

Complete guide to W&B Artifacts: creating dataset/model/result artifacts, downloading and using artifacts, model registry with staging/promotion, versioning and lineage tracking, artifact references, collaboration patterns, and best practices.
Guide to integrating W&B with popular ML frameworks: HuggingFace Transformers, PyTorch Lightning, Keras/TensorFlow, Fast.ai, XGBoost/LightGBM, PyTorch Native, and custom integrations.

Comprehensive guide to hyperparameter optimization with W&B Sweeps: search strategies (grid, random, Bayesian), parameter distributions, early termination (Hyperband), parallel execution, advanced patterns (nested/conditional parameters), and real-world examples.

properties.name: huggingface-hub
properties.author: Hugging Face
properties.description: Hugging Face Hub CLI (hf) -- search, download, and upload models and datasets, manage repos, query datasets with SQL, deploy inference endpoints, manage Spaces and buckets.

properties.name: gguf-quantization
properties.author: Orchestra Research
properties.description: GGUF format and llama.cpp quantization for efficient CPU/GPU inference. Deploying models on consumer hardware, Apple Silicon, or flexible quantization from 2-8 bit.

Advanced GGUF usage: speculative decoding, batched inference, custom model conversion, mixed/IQ quantization, memory optimization (mmap, partial GPU offload, KV cache), context management, grammar-constrained generation, LoRA integration, embedding generation, performance tuning, multi-GPU setup, custom builds.

Troubleshooting GGUF/llama.cpp: build failures, Python bindings issues, conversion issues, quantization crashes, slow generation, OOM errors, garbage output, server issues, Apple Silicon Metal issues, debugging tips.

properties.name: guidance
properties.author: Orchestra Research
properties.description: Control LLM output with regex and grammars, guarantee valid JSON/XML/code generation, enforce structured formats, and build multi-step workflows with Guidance - Microsoft Research's constrained generation framework.

Guide to configuring Guidance with different LLM backends: Anthropic Claude, OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Transformers (HuggingFace), llama.cpp. Includes backend comparison, performance tuning, memory requirements, batch processing, and advanced configuration.
Model Context Protocol -- a standard for connecting AI agents to external tools and data sources. Used by mcporter and native-mcp skills in Hermes Agent.
A commit message convention using type(scope): description format. Types include feat, fix, refactor, docs, test, ci, chore, perf, style, build, revert.
GPT-Generated Unified Format -- the standard file format for llama.cpp, enabling efficient inference on CPUs, Apple Silicon, and GPUs with flexible quantization from 2-8 bit using K-quant methods.

Reducing model precision (bits per weight) for efficient inference. Methods include K-quants (Q2_K through Q8_0), IQ quantization, importance matrix (imatrix), and alternatives like AWQ, GPTQ, HQQ, bitsandbytes.
Machine Learning Operations -- tools and frameworks for training, fine-tuning, deploying, and optimizing ML/AI models.
Systematic assessment of LLM performance using standardized benchmarks (MMLU, GSM8K, HumanEval, etc.) with reproducible evaluation methodology.
Systematic logging of ML experiment parameters, metrics, and artifacts for reproducibility, comparison, and collaboration.
Controlling LLM output to match specified patterns (regex, grammars) during token generation, ensuring valid structured outputs without post-hoc validation or retries.
GitHub's official command-line tool for interacting with GitHub. Primary method for PR workflow and repo management; skills provide curl fallback when unavailable.
C/C++ inference engine for LLMs in GGUF format. Supports CPU, Apple Silicon Metal, and NVIDIA CUDA acceleration. Includes conversion, quantization, and server tools.
Serverless GPU cloud platform for running ML workloads. Python-native infrastructure definition with auto-scaling, container caching, and web endpoints.
ML experiment tracking and MLOps platform with automatic metric logging, real-time dashboards, hyperparameter sweeps, and model registry.
EleutherAI's framework for evaluating LLMs across 60+ academic benchmarks with standardized prompts and metrics.
Modern CLI for Hugging Face Hub -- search, download, upload models/datasets, manage repos, deploy endpoints, manage Spaces.
Microsoft Research's constrained generation framework for controlling LLM output with regex, grammars, and Pythonic control flow.
Google's Tenor GIF search API. Free API key with generous rate limits.
Free, no-API-key-needed mapping APIs: Overpass API for querying map data, Nominatim for geocoding.
GitHub's REST API for repositories, pull requests, issues, CI/Actions, releases, secrets, and branch protection. Base URL: https://api.github.com. Rate limit: 5000 requests/hour authenticated.
Python library (youtube-transcript-api) for fetching YouTube video transcripts programmatically.
On-demand GPU access without infrastructure management. Pay-per-second pricing, auto-scaling, scale-to-zero. Alternative to reserved GPU instances and Kubernetes.
Automated search for optimal ML model hyperparameters using strategies like grid search, random search, and Bayesian optimization.
Centralized repository for managing ML model versions with staging, promotion, and lineage tracking. Used in W&B for governance and deployment.
Inference optimization technique using a smaller draft model to predict tokens, verified by the larger target model. Can significantly increase generation throughput.
Guidance technique that backs up one token at prompt-generation boundaries and regenerates, fixing unnatural spacing and improving output quality.

metadata.name: llama-cpp
metadata.description: Runs LLM inference on CPU, Apple Silicon, and consumer GPUs without NVIDIA hardware.

metadata.name: serving-llms-vllm
metadata.description: Serves LLMs with high throughput using vLLM's PagedAttention and continuous batching.

metadata.name: obliteratus
metadata.description: Remove refusal behaviors from open-weight LLMs using mechanistic interpretability techniques.

metadata.name: outlines
metadata.description: Guarantee valid JSON/XML/code structure during generation with Outlines structured generation library.
Specific model architectures and tools — computer vision (CLIP, SAM, Stable Diffusion), speech (Whisper), audio generation (AudioCraft), and multimodal models (LLaVA).

metadata.name: audiocraft-audio-generation
metadata.description: PyTorch library for audio generation including text-to-music (MusicGen) and text-to-sound (AudioGen).

nodes.label: CLIP
nodes.description: OpenAI's Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training model for zero-shot image classification and cross-modal retrieval, trained on 400M image-text pairs

nodes.label: Segment Anything Model (SAM)
nodes.description: Meta AI's foundation model for zero-shot image segmentation supporting point, box, and mask prompts. Extended by SAM 2 for video segmentation

nodes.label: SAM 2
nodes.description: Extension of SAM to video segmentation with streaming memory architecture

nodes.label: Grounded SAM
nodes.description: Combination of SAM with GroundingDINO for text-prompted segmentation

nodes.label: Stable Diffusion
nodes.description: Text-to-image generation model using HuggingFace Diffusers library, supporting SD 1.5, SDXL, and SD 3.0 variants with ControlNet, LoRA, and inpainting

nodes.label: Whisper
nodes.description: OpenAI's speech recognition model supporting 99 languages with 6 model sizes (tiny 39M to large 1550M params), plus turbo variant. Supports transcription and translation to English

nodes.label: DSPy
nodes.description: Stanford NLP's declarative language model programming framework (22k+ GitHub stars) for building and optimizing AI systems with modules, optimizers, and signatures

nodes.label: dspy.Predict
nodes.description: Core DSPy module for basic prediction using signatures

nodes.label: dspy.ChainOfThought
nodes.description: DSPy module that adds chain-of-thought reasoning before producing output

nodes.label: dspy.ReAct
nodes.description: DSPy module implementing Reason+Act loop with tool use

nodes.label: dspy.ProgramOfThought
nodes.description: DSPy module that generates and executes code to produce output

nodes.label: dspy.TypedPredictor
nodes.description: DSPy module with type-annotated input/output fields

nodes.label: dspy.MultiChainComparison
nodes.description: DSPy module that compares multiple reasoning chains and selects best

nodes.label: BootstrapFewShot
nodes.description: DSPy optimizer that bootstraps few-shot examples from training data

nodes.label: MIPRO
nodes.description: DSPy optimizer for joint instruction and few-shot proposal via bayesian hyperparameter search

nodes.label: BootstrapFinetune
nodes.description: DSPy optimizer that compiles few-shot demonstrations into weight updates for fine-tuning

nodes.label: COPRO
nodes.description: DSPy optimizer that generates and refines instructions using coordinate ascent

nodes.label: KNNFewShot
nodes.description: DSPy optimizer that selects few-shot examples via k-nearest-neighbors in embedding space

nodes.label: DSPy Signature
nodes.description: Declarative specification of input->output fields for DSPy modules

nodes.label: Axolotl
nodes.description: Open-source LLM fine-tuning framework with YAML config-driven approach supporting LoRA/QLoRA/DoRA adapters and multiple RL methods (DPO/IPO/KTO/ORPO/GRPO/SimPO)

nodes.label: Axolotl LoRA/QLoRA
nodes.description: LoRA and QLoRA adapter support in Axolotl for parameter-efficient fine-tuning with Triton kernel optimizations

nodes.label: Axolotl Multi-GPU Training
nodes.description: Distributed training support via DeepSpeed and FSDP2 in Axolotl for multi-node and multi-GPU setups

nodes.label: Axolotl Mixed Precision
nodes.description: Mixed precision training support: FP16, BF16, and FP8 for efficient GPU memory utilization

nodes.label: AxolotlGRPOTrainer
nodes.description: Custom GRPO trainer extending TRL's base GRPOTrainer with axolotl helpers and sequence parallelism support

nodes.label: AxolotlDPOTrainer
nodes.description: Custom DPO trainer extending TRL's base DPOTrainer with axolotl helpers for dataset tags

nodes.label: Axolotl Dataset Formats
nodes.description: Supports conversation/chat_template, instruction/alpaca, completion, input_output (template-free), pre-tokenized, and stepwise_supervised formats

nodes.label: Axolotl Chat Template Strategy
nodes.description: Uses Jinja2 chat_templates (ChatML, Gemma, tokenizer_default, custom) for formatting conversation datasets into prompts

nodes.label: GRPO/RL Training
nodes.description: Group Relative Policy Optimization implementation using TRL with customizable reward functions for math, code, and summarization tasks

nodes.label: GRPO Reward Functions
nodes.description: 20+ reward function examples for GRPO training: correctness (exact/fuzzy/numeric/code), format (XML/JSON), length, style (reasoning/citations), and combined multi-objective

nodes.label: TRL (Transformer Reinforcement Learning)
nodes.description: HuggingFace library for training transformer models with RL methods including GRPO, DPO, KTO, ORPO

nodes.label: LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation)
nodes.description: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning method used across Axolotl and GRPO training for efficient adapter-based training

nodes.label: DeepSpeed
nodes.description: Distributed training framework used by Axolotl for multi-GPU and multi-node training

nodes.label: PEFT (Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning)
nodes.description: HuggingFace library for parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods (LoRA, QLoRA, etc.) used by Axolotl

nodes.label: PyTorch
nodes.description: Deep learning framework used as foundation by all models and training frameworks in this skill set

nodes.label: HuggingFace Transformers
nodes.description: Core library for loading and using transformer models, shared dependency across CLIP, SAM, Whisper, Axolotl, and GRPO training

nodes.label: HuggingFace Diffusers
nodes.description: Library for diffusion model inference and training, primary interface for Stable Diffusion in this skill set

nodes.label: ControlNet
nodes.description: Conditional diffusion control method for Stable Diffusion enabling spatial conditioning (edges, depth, pose)

nodes.label: Stable Diffusion LoRA
nodes.description: LoRA adapter fine-tuning for Stable Diffusion models via DreamBooth and textual inversion

nodes.label: vLLM
nodes.description: High-throughput LLM serving engine integrated with Axolotl for online RL training

nodes.label: Zero-Shot Classification
nodes.description: Classification without task-specific training, core capability of CLIP models using text-image similarity

nodes.label: Image Segmentation
nodes.description: Pixel-level image understanding task, primary capability of SAM models with point/box/mask prompts

nodes.label: Speech Recognition (ASR)
nodes.description: Automatic speech recognition task, primary capability of Whisper across 99 languages

nodes.label: Text-to-Image Generation
nodes.description: Image generation from text prompts, primary capability of Stable Diffusion models

nodes.label: RLHF (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback)
nodes.description: Training paradigm using human preferences, implemented via DPO/IPO/KTO/ORPO/GRPO/SimPO in Axolotl

nodes.label: MedSAM
nodes.description: Medical image segmentation variant of SAM fine-tuned for medical imaging

nodes.label: IP-Adapter
nodes.description: Image prompt adapter for Stable Diffusion enabling image-conditioned generation

nodes.label: Sequence Parallelism
nodes.description: Distribution technique splitting sequences across GPUs for long-context training in Axolotl

nodes.label: TensorRT
nodes.description: NVIDIA inference optimization engine used for SAM acceleration

nodes.label: Online RL Methods (PPO, GRPO, RLOO, OnlineDPO)
Human Evaluation Guide for ML/AI Research
ML Paper Writing Philosophy & Best Practices
Gopen & Swan - Science of Scientific Writing
Browser LLM response None content guard
SAELens: Sparse Autoencoders for Mechanistic Interpretability
nodes.author: Orchestra Research

nodes.label: slime: LLM Post-Training Framework for RL Scaling
nodes.author: Orchestra Research

nodes.label: TorchTitan - PyTorch Native Distributed LLM Pretraining
nodes.author: Orchestra Research

hyperedges.label: Core Agent Loop
hyperedges.description: The agent loop in run_agent.py orchestrates prompt building, LLM calling, tool dispatch via model_tools/registry, and context compression in a synchronous iteration cycle.

hyperedges.label: Tool Discovery Pipeline
hyperedges.description: Tool discovery pipeline: _discover_tools() imports all tool modules triggering registry.register(), then MCP and plugin tools are discovered via their respective mechanisms.

hyperedges.label: Session Persistence Stack
hyperedges.description: Session persistence uses SQLite in WAL mode with FTS5 for full-text search, scoped to the profile via get_hermes_home(), consumed by both CLI and gateway.

hyperedges.relation: API error recovery pipeline: classify error, compute jittered backoff, track rate limits
hyperedges.label: API error recovery pipeline: classify error, compute jittered backoff, track rate limits

hyperedges.relation: Cron job execution pipeline: scheduler tick dispatches job, resolves model route, redacts script output
hyperedges.label: Cron job execution pipeline: scheduler tick dispatches job, resolves model route, redacts script output

hyperedges.relation: System prompt assembly: memory providers supply context blocks, prompt builder injects skills and context files
hyperedges.label: System prompt assembly: memory providers supply context blocks, prompt builder injects skills and context files

hyperedges.relation: HermesAgentBaseEnv orchestrates the full rollout pipeline by creating HermesAgentLoop for agent execution, then passing ToolContext to compute_reward for verification

p5.js Creative Coding Ecosystem
hyperedges.description: p5.js provides an integrated creative coding ecosystem where WebGL mode enables 3D rendering with GLSL shaders, Perlin noise drives flow fields and particle systems, and framebuffers enable post-processing effects like bloom -- all combinable within the same sketch

hyperedges.label: p5.js Deterministic Export Pipeline
hyperedges.description: p5.js sketches can be deterministically exported via Puppeteer headless capture (export-frames.js) for PNG frames, ffmpeg for MP4 encoding, CCapture.js for GIF capture, and deployed to generative art platforms (fxhash, Art Blocks) with deterministic seeding

hyperedges.label: Warm Neutral Design Family (Claude + Notion)
hyperedges.description: Claude (Anthropic) and Notion share a warm neutral design philosophy: yellow-brown undertoned grays, avoidance of cool blue-grays, generous whitespace, and both use Inter as their CDN font substitute -- creating approachable, paper-like digital experiences

hyperedges.label: Dark Background + Singular Accent Design Family (MongoDB + NVIDIA)
hyperedges.description: MongoDB and NVIDIA both use deep dark backgrounds (teal-black or pure black) with a single saturated accent color (neon green) and dark/light section alternation, creating high-contrast tech-forward experiences -- MongoDB with warm teal tones, NVIDIA with cold industrial precision

hyperedges.label: Hermes Agent Design Template System
hyperedges.description: The Hermes Agent's Popular Web Designs skill catalogs 54 design system templates, each following a 9-section structure, with proprietary fonts substituted via Google Fonts CDN, output served through cloudflared tunnels, and visually verified -- enabling faithful reproduction of any cataloged brand's design language

properties.description: vLLM serves models at high throughput, Outlines adds structured generation on top, llama.cpp provides CPU/edge alternative. All three can interoperate through shared backends.
After OBLITERATUS removes refusals, the abliterated model can be served via vLLM for production or converted to GGUF for llama.cpp edge deployment.
GGUF K-quantization tiers form a quality-size tradeoff spectrum: Q4_K_M (recommended default) -> Q5_K_M (quality critical) -> Q6_K (max quality) -> Q8_0 (near lossless).

hyperedges.description: Grounded SAM pipeline: GroundingDINO detects objects from text prompts, then SAM generates precise segmentation masks
Stable Diffusion training stack: Diffusers provides pipeline abstractions, LoRA enables efficient fine-tuning of SD models
Axolotl RLHF stack: Axolotl exposes TRL's RL training methods including GRPO with vLLM acceleration
DSPy optimization loop: Modules use Signatures to define I/O, Optimizers tune the module prompts/weights automatically
FSDP + QLoRA pipeline in Axolotl: combines model sharding with 4-bit quantized LoRA for training large models on consumer GPUs
Whisper deployment stack: faster-whisper optimizes inference via CTranslate2, LangChain enables integration into LLM application pipelines
Quantization ecosystem spanning GGUF (llama.cpp), AWQ, and GPTQ methods for model compression across different inference backends
LLM inference serving ecosystem with llama.cpp (CPU/edge), vLLM (GPU/production), and Outlines (structured generation) interconnected through shared backends and OpenAI-compatible APIs

vLLM performance optimization techniques: PagedAttention for KV cache management, continuous batching for throughput, prefix caching for repeated prompts, and speculative decoding for latency reduction

All seven major frameworks/models form the MLOps skills category covering computer vision (CLIP, SAM, Stable Diffusion), audio (Whisper), research (DSPy), and training (Axolotl, GRPO). They share common dependencies on PyTorch and HuggingFace Transformers.

LoRA (via PEFT) is the shared parameter-efficient fine-tuning technique across Axolotl (LLM training), Stable Diffusion (image generation), and GRPO/RL training. Each applies LoRA adapters to different model architectures for efficient training.

The RLHF training pipeline connects Axolotl (framework) and GRPO/RL Training (technique) through TRL (library). Axolotl extends TRL trainers (AxolotlGRPOTrainer, AxolotlDPOTrainer) with reward functions to implement various RLHF methods (DPO, GRPO, etc.).

hyperedges.relation: RLHF pipeline consists of SFT -> Reward Model training -> PPO optimization
PEFT methods family: LoRA base with QLoRA, DoRA, AdaLoRA, LoftQ as variants
TRL provides SFT, DPO, PPO, and GRPO training methods for LLM alignment
google_api_py delegates to gws_bridge_py which delegates to gws_cli
PPTX editing workflow: add_slide.py duplicates slides, clean.py removes orphaned files, pack.py repacks into .pptx
PDF extraction uses pymupdf (lightweight, text-based PDFs) or marker-pdf (OCR, scanned docs, equations). pymupdf is default; marker for OCR needs.

The godmode skill defines three attack modes: GODMODE CLASSIC (jailbreak templates), PARSELTONGUE (input obfuscation via parseltongue.py), and ULTRAPLINIAN (multi-model racing via godmode_race.py). auto_jailbreak orchestrates all three with auto-detection and config writing. load_godmode provides the loader for exec() contexts.

The research paper writing skill is a 8-phase pipeline (Setup, Literature Review, Experiment Design, Execution, Analysis, Drafting, Review, Submission). It references autoreason_methodology for iterative refinement strategy selection, conference_checklists for NeurIPS/ICML/ICLR/ACL/AAAI/COLM requirements, citation_workflow for programmatic citation verification, experiment_patterns for monitoring and evaluation protocols, and arxiv_skill for literature discovery.

The polymarket skill uses three public REST APIs (Gamma for discovery/search, CLOB for prices/orderbooks, Data for trades). The api-endpoints reference documents all endpoints with curl examples. The polymarket.py script provides a CLI that calls all three APIs for search, trending, price, book, history, and trades operations.

hyperedges.description: Plan mode creates the plan, subagent-driven-development executes it via delegate_task with two-stage review, and requesting-code-review performs the pre-commit verification pipeline. These three skills form a complete development workflow.

The research-paper-writing skill's reference documents form an interconnected guide: writing-guide covers narrative and style, reviewer-guidelines explains conference review criteria, human-evaluation covers study design, paper-types covers non-standard paper formats, and templates provides LaTeX formatting. Together they cover the full paper writing lifecycle.

These templates collectively provide LaTeX formatting for the major ML/AI conferences: NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, ACL, AAAI, and COLM. Each template enforces conference-specific page limits, anonymization rules, and formatting standards.

Software development skills form a related cluster: systematic-debugging references TDD for bug fixes, writing-plans references TDD for task design, and all three reference subagent-driven-development for delegation

Context length and output token management spans model_metadata (parse errors, probe tiers), anthropic_adapter (build kwargs with output cap clamping), and tests for Ollama num_ctx detection and context halving bugfix
Core hermes-agent infrastructure: SessionDB for session persistence, EventBridge for MCP event queuing, logging setup, with conftest providing shared test isolation fixtures

hyperedges.relation: acp_adapter is a subsystem with modules for server, session, events, tools, auth, permissions, and entry
auxiliary client resolution chain spans auxiliary_client, anthropic_adapter, CLI config, runtime provider, and vision tools
compression subsystem includes TrajectoryCompressor with its config/metrics types and runtime ContextCompressor

hyperedges.description: The memory system uses a provider pattern: MemoryProvider ABC is implemented by BuiltinMemoryProvider, Mem0MemoryProvider, and HonchoMemoryProvider. MemoryManager orchestrates multiple providers, allowing one builtin plus one external provider.

Model context length resolution uses a multi-tier pipeline: DEFAULT_CONTEXT_LENGTHS lookup, API metadata fetch with caching, models.dev registry, custom endpoint metadata, local server probing (Ollama/vLLM/LM Studio), and persistent YAML cache. The anthropic_adapter uses context metadata for thinking parameter decisions.

Prompt assembly aggregates context from multiple sources: AGENTS.md/.hermes.md/SOUL.md files (with injection scanning), skills index (with platform/conditional filtering), Nous subscription features, and Anthropic cache control markers. Skills are discovered via skill_utils and skills_tool, then indexed by skill_commands for slash-command registration.
Branch, new/reset/clear, and retry commands all manage session lifecycle through SessionDB and HermesCLI, creating, ending, or replacing sessions with conversation history management
HermesCLI resolves runtime providers through resolve_runtime_provider, supporting multiple providers (openai-codex, nous, openrouter, custom) with smart model routing for cheap/simple queries

Multiple TUI subsystems render feedback within HermesCLI: extension hooks for subclass customization, approval UI for dangerous commands, background task refresh to prevent overlap, loading indicators for slow commands, and context length warnings in the banner

HermesCLI and GatewayRunner both create and manage AIAgent instances. GatewayRunner dispatches commands received from platform adapters (TelegramAdapter). The cron scheduler independently runs AIAgent jobs via run_job(), with jobs managed by cron_jobs CRUD.

AIAgent._extract_reasoning pulls reasoning from multiple provider formats (OpenRouter reasoning_details, DeepSeek reasoning field, Moonshot reasoning_content, inline think blocks). The extracted reasoning flows through reasoning_callback to HermesCLI, which displays it via streaming deltas, preview buffering, or final box depending on /reasoning command settings.

Cron jobs are created/updated via cron_jobs, scheduled and executed by cron_scheduler using runtime_provider for credentials and AIAgent for execution. Results are delivered via send_message_tool or platform adapters, with sessions persisted in SessionDB. Skills can be attached to jobs via skills_tool, and script output is injected into prompts.

hyperedges.label: Session Lifecycle and Command Approval Flow
Matrix E2EE End-to-End Encryption Flow
hyperedges.description: Multiple test classes in test_matrix.py cover E2EE lifecycle: key export/import on disconnect, encrypted send fallback with unverified device retry, MegolmEvent buffering and decryption retry, auto-trust devices, and encrypted media handling (images, voice, documents). All gated by MATRIX_ENCRYPTION env var plumbed through gateway config.

hyperedges.label: Platform Adapter Lifecycle: Connect, Message, Reconnect, Send
hyperedges.description: Tests covering the full platform adapter lifecycle: BasePlatformAdapter message parsing and media extraction, interrupt key matching for session-scoped message queuing, /queue-based pending message storage without interrupt signals, post-completion queue consumption, and automatic reconnection watcher for failed adapters with retryable vs non-retryable error handling.

hyperedges.label: Gateway Slash Command Handler Suite
hyperedges.description: Tests for gateway slash commands: /plan loads skill and runs agent, /reasoning hot-reloads reasoning effort config and toggles display, /resume switches to a named session via SessionDB lookup, /retry replaces last user turn in transcript and returns the new response (regression fix for returning None).

hyperedges.relation: All platform adapters extend BasePlatformAdapter, sharing send_with_retry, extract_media, and SendResult patterns

Session lifecycle: SessionSource identifies a conversation, build_session_key generates a unique key, SessionStore creates/manages SessionEntry with optional auto-reset via SessionResetPolicy, persistence via SessionDB, and lifecycle hooks via invoke_hook

Message processing pipeline: GatewayRunner receives MessageEvent via platform adapters, places sentinel to prevent concurrent runs, manages session via SessionStore, runs hygiene checks using model_metadata for token estimation, and delivers responses via _send_with_retry with backoff on transient errors
gateway test suite covering all platform adapters and gateway commands
hermes CLI auth test suite covering all auth providers and credential management
multi-provider authentication system supporting OpenRouter, Anthropic, Codex, Nous, Qwen, Z.AI, Kimi, MiniMax, AI Gateway, Kilo Code, HuggingFace providers

hyperedges.description: Four test files collectively test the hermes_cli.gateway module covering systemd/launchd service management, linger status, runtime health checks, and general gateway command routing.

Three test files collectively validate hermes_cli.config: loading/saving config, env var expansion in YAML values, and structural validation of config.yaml fields like custom_providers and fallback_model.

Three test files cover provider and model integration: codex model resolution and normalization, copilot GitHub token validation and API header generation, and Gemini provider registry/credential/auto-detection.

hyperedges.relation: Model selection triggers provider resolution which reads config, resolves auth credentials, and determines API mode
Plugin system handles discovery, loading, hook invocation, tool registration, and CLI command exposure
MCP server configuration involves adding/removing servers, probing tools, interactive checklist selection, and persisting config

hyperedges.label: Setup Wizard Flow
hyperedges.description: Test files that collectively verify the hermes setup wizard: provider/model selection, non-interactive mode, OpenClaw migration, prompt menus, shell script validation, and Matrix E2EE setup.

hyperedges.label: Skills Management Tests
hyperedges.description: Test files covering the skills subsystem: configuration (disable/enable per platform), hub operations (list/check/update/install), CLI install flags (--yes/--force), skip_confirm behavior, and subparser conflict prevention.

hyperedges.label: Update and Gateway Restart
hyperedges.description: Test files covering the hermes update mechanism: git stash/restore, update check caching, gateway restart (systemd/launchd), service PID exclusion, and subprocess timeout enforcement across CLI modules.

hyperedges.relation: all implement MemoryProviderInterface with tool_schemas, prefetch, sync_turn, system_prompt_block
Daytona and Modal are interchangeable terminal backends tested via terminal_tool with TERMINAL_ENV switch
Context overflow handling: AIAgent detects 413/400 context-length errors, triggers context_compressor to compress history and retry
When fallback activates, the context compressor is updated with the fallback model credentials; after the turn, primary runtime is restored including compressor state
Context compression creates a new session; compressed messages must be flushed to new SQLite session and JSONL transcript with offset=0

Anthropic errors are classified into retryable (429/529/500), non-retryable (400), credential-refresh (401), compression-triggering (prompt-too-long), and Sonnet long-context-429 which reduces context_length instead of retrying

The browser_tool module integrates camofox mode, CDP URL resolution, SSRF protection for cloud providers, agent-browser CLI discovery, console/visibility features, session cleanup, and content guards into a unified browser automation system.

The security pipeline combines tirith security scanning, command approval flow, prompt injection prevention for cron jobs, and environment variable passthrough filtering to protect against command injection and data exfiltration.

Hermes supports multiple execution environment backends (Docker, Daytona cloud sandbox, local) that share a common base class. The code execution sandbox generates hermes_tools RPC modules that run inside these environments with filtered env vars.

hyperedges.description: These 6 test files collectively validate the file operations safety subsystem: write deny lists, device path blocking, deduplication, staleness detection, safe root sandboxing, character limits, and shell noise filtering.

These 5 test files validate the managed tool gateway infrastructure: Modal sandboxing, BrowserUse/Browserbase providers, FAL image generation, TTS/transcription gateways, environment variable isolation, and idempotency key management.

These 4 test files validate the MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration layer: dynamic tool discovery with nuke-and-repave, OAuth 2.1 PKCE authentication, server probing, tool call parser compatibility, and ManagedServer API contracts.

hyperedges.relation: four test files collectively validate the MCP tool module covering stability, structured content, core functionality, and issue-948 regression
two test files validate Modal sandbox infrastructure and snapshot isolation against the Modal SDK
send message tests cover multiple messaging platforms (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Mattermost, Matrix, HomeAssistant, DingTalk) routed through gateway platform config

The skills lifecycle involves: skills_sync provisions bundled skills, skill_manager_tool handles CRUD, skills_tool provides discovery/viewing, skills_guard provides security scanning, and skills_hub manages remote sources
Terminal execution uses multiple backends (local, SSH, modal, docker) with process_registry managing processes, tirith_security for command scanning, and threaded_process_handle for SDK adapters
Path security is enforced at multiple layers: path traversal prevention in skill_view, symlink prefix confusion fix using is_relative_to(), and structural security scanning in skills_guard
The voice mode pipeline integrates CLI voice commands, audio recording via voice_mode, speech-to-text via transcription_tools, and text-to-speech via tts_tool

The browser/web security stack chains SSRF protection (url_safety), website blocklist policy (website_policy), and browser backends (browser_tool with camofox/camofox_state) to enforce access controls on all outbound web requests

The command approval system integrates dangerous pattern detection (approval), ANSI normalization (ansi_strip), external security scanning (tirith_security), and terminal execution (terminal_tool) for pre-exec safety checks

hyperedges.label: Hermes Tool Registration Pattern
hyperedges.description: All LLM-callable tools follow a consistent pattern: define a schema (OpenAI function-calling format), implement a handler function, and register into the central tools.registry with a name, toolset, schema, handler, check_fn, and emoji.

hyperedges.label: Sandbox Security Chain
hyperedges.description: Code execution sandbox blocks secret env vars from child processes, uses env_passthrough for skill-declared variables, and redacts secrets from output via agent.redact. The terminal_tool provides the execution environment.

hyperedges.label: MCP Integration Stack
hyperedges.description: MCP tool connects to external servers via stdio or HTTP transport, discovers tools dynamically, registers them into the Hermes tool registry, uses mcp_oauth for OAuth 2.1 PKCE authentication, osv_check for malware scanning of npx/uvx packages, and auxiliary_client for server-initiated sampling LLM calls.
